title: Systems Fully Honked
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS count, COALESCE(SUM(BodyCount), 0) AS totalBodies FROM FSSDiscoveryScan WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate

details.text: {count} star system(s) fully surveyed with the Full Spectrum Scanner during the report period, revealing {totalBodies} bodies across all those systems. An FSS sweep — colloquially a 'honk' — detects every body in a system and is required before detailed scanning or probe mapping can begin.
details.help: FSSDiscoveryScan fires once per FSS honk. BodyCount is the total bodies detected in the system. summaryOnly: in a by-system report this would always return 1.

tagLines: Full Spectrum Scanner utilisation data submitted to the cartographic bureau.
You honked it. You honked every single one of them. You honk-completionist.
A honk is how you announce yourself to a new system. This is how many times you said hello.
